summary: Implemented several small scratch fixes across camera, Flat Zone, dev text, and particle code. The PR appears to resolve five scratch issues (#2550 through #2554) by making localized matching-oriented edits: introducing temporaries and pointer aliases in gmCamera, reshaping a Flat Zone JObj null check, adding dont_inline pragmas around small DevText helpers, adding stack padding and narrowing a render-priority argument, and simplifying particle bin iteration to direct array indexing.
what_changed: In `gmCamera_801A30E4`, repeated accesses to `gmCamera_80479BC8.gcus` were rewritten through local aliases: `gmCameraUnkStruct* gcus`, `s32* pxc`, and `HSD_JObj** px8`.
In `gmCamera_801A30E4`, `gmCamera_803DA6B4[gmCamera_80479BC8.gcus.xC]` became `gmCamera_803DA6B4[*pxc]`, and calls using `gcus->x4`/`*px8` replaced longer global-field expressions.
In `gmCamera_801A30E4`, `HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll(gcus->x8, JOBJ_HIDDEN)` was changed to `HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll((0, gcus->x8), JOBJ_HIDDEN)`, indicating an expression-shaping tactic for matching.
In `grFlatzone_80218260`, the local `HSD_JObj* jobj` declaration was hoisted and the nested block was collapsed into an assignment-in-condition null check: `if ((jobj = GET_JOBJ(gobj)) != NULL)`.
In `textdraw.c`, `DevText_InitPool` and `DevText_CreateCObj` were wrapped in `#pragma push`, `#pragma dont_inline on`, and `#pragma pop`.
In `DevText_Setup`, `PAD_STACK(8)` was added and the `GObj_SetupGXLink` render-priority argument was masked to `devtext_setup_render_priority & 0xFF`.

In `textlib.c`, small DevText flag helpers `DevText_ShowBackground`, `DevText_HideBackground`, `DevText_ShowText`, `DevText_HideText`, and `DevText_Erase` were marked `dont_inline` using pragma push/pop blocks.
In `hsd_8039CEAC`, the separate `bins` pointer variable and increment were removed; the loop now indexes `hsd_804D0908[i]` directly for bin head reads and comparisons.
smart_moves: Used local pointer aliases in camera code to influence generated addressing without changing the apparent runtime behavior.
Applied `#pragma dont_inline` narrowly around specific DevText helper functions rather than globally changing compiler behavior.
Used `PAD_STACK(8)` only in `DevText_Setup`, suggesting the stack-frame mismatch was localized and corrected with minimal disruption.
Masked `devtext_setup_render_priority` with `0xFF` at the callsite, preserving the intended byte-sized argument behavior for `GObj_SetupGXLink`.
Rewrote particle bin traversal from pointer walking to explicit array indexing, which can alter register allocation and address generation while keeping loop semantics recognizable.
Flattened the Flat Zone JObj block into assignment-in-condition form, a common decomp tactic when matching original control flow or variable lifetime.
decomp_lessons: For scratch resolutions, small source-shape changes can be sufficient: local aliases, declaration placement, assignment-in-condition, and direct indexing may affect generated code more than algorithmic changes.
When small helper functions are unexpectedly inlined, use scoped `#pragma push` / `#pragma dont_inline on` / `#pragma pop` around only the affected functions.
Stack mismatches can sometimes be resolved with targeted `PAD_STACK` rather than broader refactors.
If a function argument is semantically byte-sized but stored in a wider temporary, explicitly masking with `& 0xFF` may be needed to reproduce the original codegen.
Pointer-iteration and array-indexing forms are not interchangeable for matching; choosing one over the other can affect register pressure and emitted loads.
Hoisting a local declaration or shortening a nested block can be a valid matching tactic, especially around macro-returned values like `GET_JOBJ`.
naming_conventions: Existing project-style symbol names are preserved: `gmCamera_801A30E4`, `grFlatzone_80218260`, `DevText_*`, and `hsd_8039CEAC`.
Temporary names in scratch code are terse and structure-derived, such as `gcus`, `pxc`, and `px8`, reflecting pointer aliases to fields rather than newly understood semantic names.
The diff does not provide evidence of new public naming conventions or renamed symbols.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduce pointer aliases to global struct fields to alter load/store sequences: `gmCameraUnkStruct* gcus = &gmCamera_80479BC8.gcus` and `s32* pxc = &gcus->xC`.
Use pointer-to-field aliasing for JObj access: `HSD_JObj** px8 = &gcus->x8` followed by `*px8` in the child lookup call.
Use the comma operator in an argument expression, `(0, gcus->x8)`, as a deliberate source-shaping trick.
Hoist declarations and combine assignment with null check to match expected control flow: `(jobj = GET_JOBJ(gobj)) != NULL`.
Apply scoped `#pragma dont_inline on` to prevent inlining of small DevText helpers.
Insert `PAD_STACK(8)` to correct stack layout in `DevText_Setup`.
Mask a call argument with `& 0xFF` to force byte-width behavior or codegen.
Prefer direct array indexing `hsd_804D0908[i]` over maintaining and incrementing a `bins` pointer when that form better matches.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The PR body explicitly checked off that it resolves #2550, #2551, #2552, #2553, and #2554.
follow_up_queries: Look up issues #2550 through #2554 to map each scratch to the exact function and matching problem.
Check whether the comma-expression use `(0, gcus->x8)` in `gmCamera_801A30E4` corresponds to a known compiler-codegen workaround in this project.
Search for other DevText functions requiring `#pragma dont_inline` or `PAD_STACK` to keep interface text matching consistent.
Compare `hsd_8039CEAC` before and after matching output to determine why direct indexing of `hsd_804D0908[i]` was preferable to pointer walking.
